fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of document that you are about to create.

fields.name: Buy-from Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the vendor who delivered the items.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the document.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of this line.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line type.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a general ledger account, item, resource, additional cost, or fixed asset, depending on the contents of the Type field.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the location where you want the items to be placed when they are received.

fields.name: Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the inventory posting group or fixed asset posting group associated with the item or fixed asset on the line.
If the line contains an item, the program automatically retrieves the inventory posting group from the Item table. The program uses the inventory posting group to find the inventory account for the line.
If the line contains a fixed asset, the FA posting group is copied from the FA Depreciation Book table. The fixed asset posting group determines a number of accounts which will be posted to.

fields.name: Expected Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date you expect the items to be available in your warehouse.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the blanket purchase order.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies information in addition to the description.

fields.name: Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the item or resource's unit of measure, such as piece or hour.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the purchase order line.

fields.name: Outstanding Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units on the order line have not yet been received.

fields.name: Qty. to Invoice
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity that remains to be invoiced. It is calculated as Quantity - Qty. Invoiced.

fields.name: Qty. to Receive
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of items that remains to be received.

fields.name: Direct Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the price of one unit of what you're buying.

fields.name: Unit Cost (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost, in LCY, of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: VAT %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the VAT percentage that should be used.
The program automatically retrieves the VAT % from the General Posting Setup table when you fill in the Gen. Bus. Posting Group Code and Gen. Prod. Posting Group Code fields.
If the VAT Calculation Type field contains the Reverse Charge VAT option, the VAT % field is not filled in. In this case, the line refers to business between your country/region and another EU-country/region, and VAT will not be calculated on the invoice. However, when you post the invoice, the VAT amount is calculated and added to the domestic sales VAT and to the domestic purchase VAT. Thus, the calculated VAT does not affect the VAT payable.

fields.name: Line Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount percentage that is granted for the item on the line.

fields.name: Line Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount amount that is granted for the item on the line.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of amounts in the Line Amount field on the purchase order lines.

fields.name: Amount Including VAT
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Unit Price (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the price, in LCY, of one unit of the item or resource. You can enter a price manually or have it entered according to the Price/Profit Calculation field on the related card.

fields.name: Allow Invoice Disc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the invoice line is included when the invoice discount is calculated.

fields.name: Gross Weight
fields.tooltip: Specifies the gross weight of one unit of the item. In the purchase statistics window, the gross weight on the line is included in the total gross weight of all the lines for the particular purchase document.

fields.name: Net Weight
fields.tooltip: Specifies the net weight of one unit of the item. In the purchase statistics window, the net weight on the line is included in the total net weight of all the lines for the particular purchase document.

fields.name: Units per Parcel
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of units per parcel of the item. In the purchase statistics window, the number of units per parcel on the line helps to determine the total number of units for all the lines for the particular purchase document.

fields.name: Unit Volume
fields.tooltip: Specifies the volume of one unit of the item. In the purchase statistics window, the volume of one unit of the item on the line is included in the total volume of all the lines for the particular purchase document.

fields.name: Appl.-to Item Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item ledger entry that the document or journal line is applied to.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Job No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project.

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the item's last purchase cost that includes indirect costs, such as freight that is associated with the purchase of the item.

fields.name: Outstanding Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount for the items on the order that have not yet been received. The amount is shown in the currency of the purchase document.
The program calculates the outstanding amount as Amount Including VAT * Outstanding Quantity / Quantity.
The program automatically updates this field when the contents of the Quantity field changes or the purchase line is posted.

fields.name: Qty. Rcd. Not Invoiced
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many units, such as pieces, of the ordered item have been received but not yet invoiced.
The program calculates the quantity as the difference between the contents of the Qty. to Receive and Qty. to Invoice fields.
Each time you change the Quantity field or post the line, the program will automatically update this field.
You cannot delete a line that contains a quantity of items that have been received but not invoiced.

fields.name: Amt. Rcd. Not Invoiced
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount for the ordered items that have been received but not yet invoiced. The amount is shown in the currency of the purchase document.
The program calculates the amount as Amount Including VAT * Qty. Rcd. Not Invoiced / Quantity.

fields.name: Quantity Received
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as received.

fields.name: Quantity Invoiced
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as invoiced.

fields.name: Receipt No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of an associated receipt.
This field is used only on invoice lines.
The program automatically retrieves the number from the list of posted purchase receipts if you fill in the line using the Get Receipt Lines function.

fields.name: Receipt Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of an associated receipt line.
This field is used only on invoice lines.
The program automatically retrieves the number from the list of posted purchase receipts when you choose Functions, Get Receipt.

fields.name: Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the order number this line is associated with.

fields.name: Profit %
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Pay-to Vendor No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the vendor who will invoice the items on the purchase line.
The program automatically retrieves the vendor number from the Pay-to Vendor No. field on the purchase header.

fields.name: Inv. Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the invoice discount amount for the line.

fields.name: Vendor Item No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies either the vendor's item number for the item or the item number in your item table.
For the field to be filled in with the vendor's item number, the Vendor No. and Vendor Item No. fields in the item table must be filled in. If both fields are not filled in, the program will automatically enter the item number from your item table.

fields.name: Sales Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a sales order that will be shipped directly to the customer (a drop shipment) using this purchase order.

fields.name: Sales Order Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a sales order line containing items to be shipped directly to the customer (a drop shipment).

fields.name: Drop Shipment
fields.tooltip: Specifies if your vendor ships the items directly to your customer.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's or customer's trade type to link transactions made for this business partner with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: VAT Calculation Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies which VAT calculation type will be used when this purchase line is posted.
The program fills in the field automatically by using the VAT Bus. Posting Group and VAT Prod. Posting Group fields.
The VAT calculation type determines how the VAT will be calculated when this line is posted. There are four different types:

Normal VAT

With this option, the program calculates VAT for the amount on this line and creates a VAT ledger entry including the amount.


Reverse Charge VAT

This option is used when the line involves a purchase from another EU country/region, where the seller does not collect VAT, therefore the purchaser must compute and settle accounts with the tax authorities.
This option has no effect on your company's financial statements. When you purchase goods, the program will calculate the VAT amount and debit the purchase VAT account and credit the reverse charge VAT account.


Full VAT

This option is used when the amount you are posting on this line consists entirely of VAT. This option can be useful if, for example, you make a corrective entry in order to correct a VAT calculation error.


Sales Tax

This option is used if the program must calculate US sales tax instead of VAT.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the type of transaction used for the items in this line. To see the transaction type codes that have been set up in the Transaction Type table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the transaction type code from the purchase header. The field is blank if no code has been entered on the purchase header.
You can change the contents of the field on individual purchase lines. This information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the transport method used for the items in this line. To see the transport method codes that have been set up in the Transport Method table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the transport method from the purchase header. The field is blank if no transport method has been entered on the purchase header.
You can change the contents of the field on individual purchase lines. This information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Attached to Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number to which this purchase line is attached.

fields.name: Entry Point
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code of the port of entry where the items pass into your country/region.
The program retrieves the code automatically from the Entry Point field on the purchase header.
The code is used for reporting to INTRASTAT and will appear on the Intrastat Journal.
To see the entry points that you can choose, click the field.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the area code for the vendor. To see the area codes that have been set up in the Area table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the area code from the purchase header. The field is blank if no code has been entered on the purchase header.
You can change the contents of the field on individual purchase lines. This information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the type of transaction specification used for the items in this line. To see the transaction specification codes that have been set up in the Transaction Specification table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the transaction specification code from the purchase header. The field is blank if no code has been entered on the purchase header.
You can change the contents of the field on individual purchase lines. This information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax area that is used to calculate and post sales tax.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies that purchases from the vendor on the purchase header are liable for sales tax.

fields.name: Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax group code for the tax detail entry.

fields.name: Use Tax
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the purchase is subject to use tax. Use tax is a sales tax that is paid on items that are purchased by a company and are used by that company instead of being sold to a customer.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's VAT specification to link transactions made for this vendor with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: VAT Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT product posting group. Links business transactions made for the item, resource, or G/L account with the general ledger, to account for VAT amounts resulting from trade with that record.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the currency of the amounts on the purchase line.

fields.name: Outstanding Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount for the items on the order that have not yet been received in LCY.

fields.name: Amt. Rcd. Not Invoiced (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum, in LCY, for items that have been received but have not yet been invoiced. The value in the Amt. Rcd. Not Invoiced (LCY) field is used for entries in the Purchase Line table of document type Order to calculate and update the contents of this field.

fields.name: Reserved Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many item units on this line have been reserved.

fields.name: Blanket Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the blanket order that the record originates from.

fields.name: Blanket Order Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the blanket order line that the record originates from.

fields.name: VAT Base Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total net amount of the purchase line (excluding VAT).

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: System-Created Entry
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Line Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the net amount, excluding any invoice discount amount, that must be paid for products on the line.

fields.name: VAT Difference
fields.tooltip:
Shows the difference between the VAT amount that is automatically calculated and the VAT amount that you entered manually in the VAT Amount field.
The VAT difference displayed here is distributed proportionally between purchase lines with the same VAT identifier.

fields.name: Inv. Disc. Amount to Invoice
fields.tooltip: Specifies the actual invoice discount amount that will be posted for the line on the invoice.

fields.name: VAT Identifier
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Automatically copies the value from the VAT Identifier field in the VAT Posting Setup window.

fields.name: IC Partner Ref. Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item or account in your IC partner's company that corresponds to the item or account on the line.

fields.name: IC Partner Reference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the IC partner. If the line is being sent to one of your intercompany partners, this field is used together with the IC Partner Ref. Type field to indicate the item or account in your partner's company that corresponds to the line.

fields.name: Prepayment %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment percentage to use to calculate the prepayment for purchases.

fields.name: Prepmt. Line Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment amount of the line in the currency of the purchase document if a prepayment percentage is specified for the purchase line.

fields.name: Prepmt. Amt. Inv.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment amount that has already been invoiced to the customer for this purchase line.

fields.name: Prepmt. Amt. Incl. VAT
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Prepayment Amount
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Prepmt. VAT Base Amt.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total prepayment amount for this purchase line (excluding VAT).

fields.name: Prepayment VAT %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the VAT percentage that should be used for prepayment invoices.
The program automatically retrieves the VAT % from the General Posting Setup table when you fill in the Gen. Bus. Posting Group and Gen. Prod. Posting Group fields.
If the VAT Calculation Type field contains the Reverse Charge VAT option, the VAT % field will not be filled in. In this case, the line refers to business between your country/region and another EU-country/region, and VAT will not be calculated on the prepayment invoice. However, when you post the prepayment invoice, the program creates a VAT entry with information about reverse charge VAT and the VAT base (the amount used to calculate the VAT amount).

fields.name: Prepmt. VAT Calc. Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies which VAT calculation type will be used when a prepayment invoice for this purchase line is posted.
The program fills in the field automatically by using the VAT Bus. Posting Group and VAT Prod. Posting Group fields.
The VAT calculation type determines how the VAT will be calculated when the prepayment invoice is posted. There are three different VAT types and one type for use with sales tax:
Normal VAT
This option is used to calculate VAT for the amount on this line and to create a VAT ledger entry including the amount.
Reverse Charge VAT
This option is used when the line involves a sale to another EU country/region, where the seller does not collect VAT because the purchaser must compute and settle accounts with the tax authorities.
Full VAT
This option cannot be used for prepayment.
Sales Tax
This option is used only if the program is set up to handle US sales tax. The option directs the program to retrieve information from specific sales tax tables and then calculate sales tax instead of VAT.

fields.name: Prepayment VAT Identifier
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Automatically copies the value from the VAT Identifier field in the VAT Posting Setup window.

fields.name: Prepayment Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the tax area code of the vendor.
The program automatically retrieves the code from the Tax Area Code field on the purchase header.
The program will use the tax area code, in combination with the tax group code on the purchase line, to determine the sales tax % and the G/L accounts for sales tax posting in connection with prepayments to vendors.
Normally, you should not change the content of this field but it may be necessary in special cases where the default code is not appropriate.

fields.name: Prepayment Tax Liable
fields.tooltip:
Specifies that purchases from the vendor on the purchase header are liable to sales tax, and sales tax will be calculated on prepayments for this purchase line. If the field is empty, no sales tax will be calculated for prepayments.
The program automatically retrieves the code from the Tax Liable field on the purchase header.

fields.name: Prepayment Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the tax group code of the item or general ledger account on this line.
The program automatically retrieves the code from the Tax Group Code field on the item card or the G/L account card when the No. field on this purchase line is filled in.
The program will use the tax group code, in combination with the tax area code on the purchase line, to determine the sales tax % and the G/L accounts for sales tax posting in connection with prepayments to vendors.
Normally, you should not change the content of this field, but it may be necessary in special cases where the default code is not appropriate.

fields.name: Prepmt Amt to Deduct
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment amount that has already been deducted from ordinary invoices posted for this purchase order line.

fields.name: Prepmt Amt Deducted
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment amount that has already been deducted from ordinary invoices posted for this purchase order line.

fields.name: Prepayment Line
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Prepmt. Amount Inv. Incl. VAT
fields.tooltip:
Shows the prepayment amount including VAT that has already been invoiced from the vendor for this purchase line. The amount is displayed in the currency of the purchase document.
You cannot change the content of this field. If you want to void the prepayment, you must post a Prepayment Credit Memo for the purchase line.

fields.name: Prepmt. Amount Inv. (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment amount that has already been invoiced to the vendor for this purchase line. The amount is displayed in the local currency of the purchase document.

fields.name: IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the intercompany partner that the transaction is related to if the entry was created from an intercompany transaction.

fields.name: Prepmt. VAT Amount Inv. (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the VAT amount that has already been invoiced to the vendor for this purchase line.
The amount is displayed in the currency of the purchase document.

fields.name: Prepayment VAT Difference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the difference between the prepayment VAT amount that is automatically calculated and the prepayment VAT amount that you entered manually in the prepayment VAT amount field.

fields.name: Prepmt VAT Diff. to Deduct
fields.tooltip: Shows the prepayment VAT difference that will be deducted from the next ordinary invoice for this line in the currency of the purchase order.

fields.name: Prepmt VAT Diff. Deducted
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment VAT difference that has already been deducted from ordinary invoices posted for this purchase order line in the currency of the purchase order.

fields.name: Outstanding Amt. Ex. VAT (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount excluding VAT for the items on the order that have not yet been received in LCY.

fields.name: A. Rcd. Not Inv. Ex. VAT (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount excluding VAT for the items on the order that have been received in LCY, but not yet invoiced.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Job Task No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project task.

fields.name: Job Line Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of planning line that was created when the project ledger entry is posted from the purchase line. If the field is empty, no planning lines were created for this entry.

fields.name: Job Unit Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sales price per unit that applies to the item or general ledger expense that will be posted.

fields.name: Job Total Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the gross amount of the line that the purchase line applies to.

fields.name: Job Line Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line amount of the project ledger entry that is related to the purchase line.

fields.name: Job Line Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line discount amount of the project ledger entry that is related to the purchase line.

fields.name: Job Line Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line discount percentage of the project ledger entry that is related to the purchase line.

fields.name: Job Unit Price (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sales price per unit that applies to the item or general ledger expense that will be posted.

fields.name: Job Total Price (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the gross amount of the line, in the local currency.

fields.name: Job Line Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line amount of the project ledger entry that is related to the purchase line.

fields.name: Job Line Disc. Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line discount amount of the project ledger entry that is related to the purchase line.

fields.name: Job Currency Factor
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Job Currency Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the currency code if the job that the purchase line applies to is set up in a foreign currency.
The program uses the Job Currency Code to calculate the cost and sales values of the purchase line in the currency specified for the job.

fields.name: Job Planning Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the project planning line number to which the usage should be linked when the Project Journal is posted. You can only link to Project Planning Lines that have the Apply Usage Link option enabled.

fields.name: Job Remaining Qty.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity that remains to complete a project.

fields.name: Job Remaining Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the item that remains to complete a job, expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Deferral Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the deferral template that governs how expenses paid with this purchase document are deferred to the different accounting periods when the expenses were incurred.

fields.name: Returns Deferral Start Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date of the returns deferral period.

fields.name: Selected Alloc. Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the allocation account number that will be used to distribute the amounts during the posting process.

fields.name: Matched Order Lines
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of purchase order lines to which this line is matched.

fields.name: Matched Inv./Cr. Memo Lines
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of purchase invoice lines to which this purchase order line is matched.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin where the items are picked or put away.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: If you have filled in the Purch. Unit of Measure field on the item card and entered a quantity for the current item in the Qty. of Base Unit of Measure per Unit field, the program automatically fills in this field when you enter the item number on the line.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The field shows the Quantity expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Outstanding Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the outstanding quantity expressed in the base units of measure.

fields.name: Qty. to Invoice (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The field shows the Qty. to Invoice expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Qty. to Receive (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The field shows the Qty. to Receive expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Qty. Rcd. Not Invoiced (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The field shows the Qty. Rcd. Not Invoiced expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Qty. Received (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The field shows the Quantity Received expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Qty. Invoiced (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The field shows the Quantity Invoiced expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reserved quantity of the item expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: FA Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the FA posting date if you have selected Fixed Asset in the Type field for this line.

fields.name: FA Posting Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that will be used on related fixed asset ledger entries.

fields.name: Depreciation Book Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the depreciation book to which the line will be posted if you have selected Fixed Asset in the Type field for this line.

fields.name: Salvage Value
fields.tooltip:
This field can only be filled in if you have selected the Acquisition option in the FA Posting Type field. In this case, enter the salvage value for the fixed asset in the field.
Normally, the salvage value must be entered as a negative amount.
Entering a salvage value at the same time as the acquisition cost is especially important if you also enter a check mark in the Depr. Acquisition Cost field. In this case, the depreciation basis will be: Acquisition Cost minus Salvage Value.

fields.name: Depr. until FA Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies if depreciation was calculated until the FA posting date of the line.

fields.name: Depr. Acquisition Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies if, when this line was posted, the additional acquisition cost posted on the line was depreciated in proportion to the amount by which the fixed asset had already been depreciated.

fields.name: Maintenance Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a maintenance code if you have selected the Maintenance option in the FA Posting Type field.
To see the codes that have been set up in the Maintenance table, click the field.

fields.name: Insurance No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the insurance number to post an insurance coverage entry to.

fields.name: Budgeted FA No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a fixed asset with the Budgeted Asset check box selected. When you post the journal or document line, an additional entry is created for the budgeted fixed asset where the amount has the opposite sign.

fields.name: Duplicate in Depreciation Book
fields.tooltip: Specifies a depreciation book code if you want the journal line to be posted to that depreciation book, as well as to the depreciation book in the Depreciation Book Code field.

fields.name: Use Duplication List
fields.tooltip: Specifies, if the type is Fixed Asset, that information on the line is to be posted to all the assets defined depreciation books.

fields.name: Responsibility Center
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the responsibility center that serves the vendor on this purchase document.

fields.name: Item Category Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the item category code for the item.
You can use item categories to group default item setup values to make creating new items easier. An item category code specifies the following values:

Default general product posting group.


Default inventory posting group.


Default VAT product posting group.


Default costing method.


To see or select item categories in the Item Category window, click the drop-down arrow in the field.

fields.name: Nonstock
fields.tooltip: Specifies that this item is a catalog item.

fields.name: Purchasing Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the purchasing code associated with the purchase line.

fields.name: Special Order
fields.tooltip:
Specifies that the item on this purchase line is a special order item.
The program automatically fills in this field, if the code you enter in the Purchase Code field has been set up as a special order purchasing code.
A special order is an item that is purchased from a vendor for a specific customer. The item is shipped from the vendor to your warehouse and then to your customer.

fields.name: Special Order Sales No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the special order sales order that created this purchase order.

fields.name: Special Order Sales Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number of the special order sales order that created this purchase order.

fields.name: Item Reference No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the referenced item number.

fields.name: Whse. Outstanding Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units on the purchase order line remain to be handled in warehouse documents.

fields.name: Completely Received
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether all the items on the line have been shipped and received by the customer.

fields.name: Requested Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that you want the vendor to deliver to the ship-to address. The value in the field is used to calculate the latest date you can order the items to have them delivered on the requested receipt date. If you do not need delivery on a specific date, you can leave the field blank.

fields.name: Promised Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that the vendor has promised to deliver the order.

fields.name: Lead Time Calculation
fields.tooltip: Specifies a date formula for the amount of time it takes to replenish the item.

fields.name: Inbound Whse. Handling Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the time it takes to make items part of available inventory, after the items have been posted as received.

fields.name: Planned Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the item is planned to arrive in inventory. Forward calculation: planned receipt date = order date + vendor lead time (per the vendor calendar and rounded to the next working day in first the vendor calendar and then the location calendar). If no vendor calendar exists, then: planned receipt date = order date + vendor lead time (per the location calendar). Backward calculation: order date = planned receipt date - vendor lead time (per the vendor calendar and rounded to the previous working day in first the vendor calendar and then the location calendar). If no vendor calendar exists, then: order date = planned receipt date - vendor lead time (per the location calendar).

fields.name: Order Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the order was created.

fields.name: Allow Item Charge Assignment
fields.tooltip: Specifies that you can assign item charges to this line.

fields.name: Qty. to Assign
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item charge will be assigned to the line.

fields.name: Qty. Assigned
fields.tooltip: Specifies how much of the item charge that has been assigned.

fields.name: Return Qty. to Ship
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of items that remains to be shipped.

fields.name: Return Qty. to Ship (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Return Qty. to Ship on the line, expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Return Qty. Shipped Not Invd.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many units of the returned item have been returned to the vendor, but not yet invoiced.
The program updates the field when you post. It calculates the contents of the field as the difference between the Return Qty. Shipped and Quantity Invoiced fields.
You cannot delete a line that contains a quantity of returned items that have been shipped but not invoiced.

fields.name: Ret. Qty. Shpd Not Invd.(Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Return Qty. Shipped Not Invd. on the line, expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Return Shpd. Not Invd.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the value of the items that you have shipped as returns to the vendor. The items have been returned, but they are not yet posted as invoiced.
The amount is in the same currency as the purchase document.

fields.name: Return Shpd. Not Invd. (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the value in LCY of the items returned to the vendor. The items have not been invoiced.

fields.name: Return Qty. Shipped
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as shipped.

fields.name: Return Qty. Shipped (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of items returned to the vendor. The quantity is expressed in the base unit of measure.

fields.name: Item Charge Qty. to Handle
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many items the item charge will be assigned to on the line. It can be either equal to Qty. to Assign or to zero. If it is zero, the item charge will not be assigned to the line.

fields.name: Non-Deductible VAT Base
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of VAT that is not deducted due to the type of goods or services purchased.

fields.name: Non-Deductible VAT Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the transaction for which VAT is not applied, due to the type of goods or services purchased.

fields.name: Return Shipment No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number that is assigned to the return shipment when you post the purchase order.

fields.name: Return Shipment Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number that is assigned to the return shipment line when you post the purchase order.

fields.name: Return Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code explaining why the item was returned.

fields.name: Attached Doc Count
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of attachments.

fields.name: Attached Lines Count
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of non-inventory product lines attached to the purchase line.

fields.name: Over-Receipt Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies over-receipt quantity.

fields.name: Over-Receipt Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies over-receipt code.

fields.name: No. of Fixed Asset Cards
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of fixed assets that is being purchased.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the items indirect cost as an absolute amount. The amount is added to the items unit cost to reflect indirect cost or overhead, such as freight and warehouse handling that are associated with the purchase of the item.

Additional Information

The value of the Overhead Rate field on the item card is copied to purchase lines and is used to calculate the value in the Unit Cost (LCY) field on a purchase line.
Note


The Planning Flexibility field is related to the Indirect Cost % field where it defines the overhead as a percentage of the value in the Direct Unit Cost field on the purchase line.

fields.name: MPS Order
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether the purchase order line is an MPS order, that is, whether it is linked to a production forecast or a sales order. If so, the program places a check mark in the field.
The user can also enter a check mark in the field when manually editing the line.

fields.name: Planning Flexibility
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the supply represented by this line is considered by the planning system when calculating action messages.

fields.name: Safety Lead Time
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a date formula, which is used as a buffer period in case of delays in the purchase replenishment lead time. When calculating order and order proposal lines, the program will add the safety lead time to the lead time. This is true for forward and backward scheduled orders. The specific date fields that are affected on the purchase order (or order proposal) line are as follows:
Planned Receipt Date + Safety Lead Time + Inbound Warehouse Handling Time = Expected Receipt Date.
Note that the program copies the field from the Stockkeeping Unit card, if one exists, or else the Item card.
